verifier_issues_summary.description: Could not open/inspect .xlsx formulas programmatically (openpyxl unavailable). Validation relied on incident_review.md and trace computed values; numeric consistency was confirmed but byte-level formula presence in the workbook could not be independently verified.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

extra_model_fields.notes: The grader's limitations and recommendations are supported by the available previews and trace: the numeric computations match and the primary shortcoming is the absence of an explicit budget-to-mitigation numeric mapping and inability to inspect .xlsx formulas programmatically. No unsupported claims or hidden-reference leakage were found.
passed_checks: artifact presence verified for abr_qoe_attribution.xlsx and incident_review.md
artifact content preview for incident_review.md parsed and matches trace computed values
per-record arithmetic validated against documented formulas (stress_score and predicted_stress)
residual logic and status assignments (confirmed_issue/needs_review/monitor) are consistent with supplied thresholds and computed residual_pct
score provenance consistent: deterministic artifact_contract precheck = 1.0; model semantic assessment lowered final_score to 0.9 with documented rationale
extra_model_fields.reproducibility_notes: All numeric computed values (stress_score, predicted_stress, residual_pct, monotone fit intercept/slope, R^2) are present in the trace 'computed' block and in incident_review.md and were independently re-computed from the reported input values. Reproduction is possible from those published numbers. However, exact Excel cell formulas could not be inspected due to missing openpyxl, so reproduction from the raw .xlsx formulas is not verifiable in this environment.

extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: Evidence is grounded in the incident_review.md preview and the trace; the workbook artifact exists but could not be programmatically opened (openpyxl_unavailable) so formula-level verification is unverifiable from the .xlsx. Internal score fields are inconsistent: artifact_contract/score fields report 1.0 while final_score/model_score report 0.9. No hidden-reference leakage detected and no unsupported analytic claims were found beyond the inability to validate workbook formulas and the missing explicit sensitivity-analysis table.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

issues.detail: attempts/revised/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] could not be parsed (parse_status: openpyxl_unavailable); grader relied on incident_review.md for formula-level claims, which are present in the text preview but the workbook formulas are not directly visible.
issues.title: unverifiable_workbook_formulas

issues.detail: The result contains multiple score fields that disagree (e.g. 'score' and 'artifact_contract_score' == 1.0 vs 'final_score' and 'model_score' == 0.9). Clarify which field is authoritative and ensure consistent reporting.
issues.title: internal_score_inconsistency

recommended_fixes: Provide a workbook preview with visible formulas (or export computed columns as CSV) so formulas can be audited programmatically, or embed the exact formula cells in the incident_review.md.
Harmonize reported score fields (final_score, score, model_score, artifact_contract_score) and document the scoring derivation so consumers can reconcile deterministically.
